docs.title: Features (v0.8.4)
docs.text:
• Visual Lego brick IDE (no CLI for end users)
  • Immortal Bootloader — flash firmware once, I2C auto-discovery forever
  • UtahClaw canvas — offline Llama-3 vibe-coding + serial auto-heal
  • Omniscient deck — driverless Espressif USB scan + live sensor cards
  • Omega defense stack — jitter, mesh mirror, PSRAM vault, chrono/tensor/lazarus modules
  • Sovereign edge tier — phonon mask, IRAM matrix compute, swarm conslation frames
  • Reality substrate — Akashic RF storage, chrono-kinetic, mnemonic hive, biosymmetric, spatial, causal, eigen, genesis
  • Field graph compiler — nodes/bindings sanctum projects → display intents
  • Formal typestate enforcement and m5-secure anti-replay wire fences
  • Real-time terminal state dashboard (enable_dashboard=True)

  • Safety validation, agentic remediation, HCP registry
  • Starter templates and save/open .flux.json projects

docs.title: m5-utah Architecture
docs.text: Utah Flux Studio / m5-utah implements an intent-first control plane for M5Stack devices. Host-side Python orchestration compiles conversational and declarative intents into wire formats; firmware on the ESP32 executes them through a thin boundary (see ADR 0001 and ADR 0002).

docs.title: System overview
docs.text:
Host (Python)                    Serial / Fluxwire              Firmware (ESP32)
─────────────────                ─────────────────              ─────────────────
IntentController  ──JSON/binary──►  Cross-core pipe  ──►  Registry + units
FluxGraph         ◄──telemetry────   Hamming ECC       ◄──  Telemetry + events
DriverRegistry                     Scheduler compiler

docs.title: m5-kernel: dual-core runtime (Features 48–49)
docs.text:
The Central Processing Registry Core (M5Kernel) partitions work across ESP32 cores:

Core Role Components Core 0 Protocol engine Serial ingest, binary frame assembly, #M/##/0xDE demux, lock-free ring push Core 1 Orchestration engine Pipe drain, intent dispatch, registry hot-reload, telemetry, UI events

setup() initializes M5 hardware, registry runtime, and calls M5Kernel::start(). The Arduino loop() task suspends; all application work runs in the pinned Core 1 orchestration task.

docs.title: Resource-aware orchestration (Feature 49)
docs.text:
ResourceOrchestrator evaluates heap, handle-pool, and bus pressure each orchestration tick:

Pressure Behavior Nominal All intents processed normally Elevated Compaction triggers earlier; telemetry reports pressure level Critical Non-critical JSON intents deferred; registry, JIT, and binary fast-paths still allowed

Host-side HostResourceOrchestrator mirrors firmware pressure and can defer non-critical transmits before they hit the wire.

docs.title: Secure wire anti-replay fences (m5-secure, Feature 67)
docs.text:
[Intent compile] → [typestate.py validate] → [secure_wire.py sequence token]
        → Core 0 CrossCorePipe (#A demux) → SecureWireFence monotonic check
        → ring buffer → Core 1 SecureWireDecoder apply
  • Host SecureWireEncoder assigns monotonic sequence IDs to outbound ## / #P fast-path frames
  • Wire magic #A (0x23 0x41) — distinct from #S stream frames (0x23 0x53)
  • Firmware drops replayed or out-of-order packets before Core 1 dispatch; emits security_alarm JSON

docs.title: Immortal Bootloader and autonomic discovery (v0.8)
docs.text:
Flash firmware once. The device becomes a permanent intent VM:

  • Core 0 ImmortalDiscovery sweeps Grove Port A I2C and emits JSON discovery events
  • utah-flux-omniscient auto-locks Espressif USB (VID 303A) and streams units to the GUI
  • utah-claw-studio runs UtahClaw locally via Ollama for vibe-coding and error auto-heal
pip install -e "host[daemon]"    # Omniscient OS
pip install -e "host[claw]"      # UtahClaw + Ollama
utah-flux-omniscient             # http://127.0.0.1:8000
utah-claw-studio                 # http://127.0.0.1:8024  (utah_studio.html)

See docs/en/utah-claw-studio.md and docs/en/intent-resolution-canvas.md.

docs.title: Omega defense stack (v0.8.2)
docs.text:
Six firmware modules harden CoreS3 against timing side-channels, single-node failure, and flash forensics:

Module Role StochasticShield ADC + TRNG jitter on registry dispatch MeshStateMirror ESP-NOW 10ms Byzantine vectors + DeepSleep handoff AmnesiaKernel PSRAM ephemeral vault + IMU tamper wipe ChronoScheduler 100µs speculative task commits TensorVoidLinkage IRAM 2-bit latent scoring LazarusDaemon RTC resurrection telemetry

Integrated via omegaDefenseInit() / omegaDefenseTick(). Telemetry includes omega_* metrics. See omega-defense-stack.md and ADR 0045.

docs.title: Field graph compiler (v0.8.2)
docs.text: Sanctum-style projects (nodes, bindings) compile through host/utah_flux/field_compiler.py — bindings stay host-side; display elements ship to firmware. See field-graph-compiler.md.

docs.title: Sovereign edge tier (v0.8.3)
docs.text:
Module Role AcousticMask Core 0 I2S Brownian phonon DMA mask MatrixCompute IRAM cache-bounded telematic scoring SwarmSoul ExecutionFrame ESP-NOW conslation (shared mesh)

See sovereign-edge-tier.md and ADR 0046.

docs.title: Reality substrate (v0.8.4)
docs.text: Eight Class-1 modules: Akashic RF storage, ChronoKinetic actuation, MnemonicProxy hive, BiosymmetricBus, SpatialUI, CausalDebugger, EigenStateCompiler, GenesisProtocol. See reality-substrate.md and ADR 0047.

docs.title: WebUSB vibe gateway (Feature 50)
docs.text:
The zero-install browser IDE (vibe_server.py on port 8023) closes the end-user loop:

Browser WebSerial  ──►  /compile_vibe  ──►  vibe_pipeline  ──►  ## binwire or JSON line  ──►  device
Natural language         validate +            FastPathSerializer              CrossCorePipe demux
                         resource preflight

GPIO prompts (e.g. "blink pin 10 at 50Hz") compile to fast_track_gpio intents and emit fixed-width ## frames without ArduinoJson on the device.

docs.title: Dual-core non-blocking harness (Feature 52)
docs.text:
Production firmware runs a strictly partitioned FreeRTOS layout — not a single-threaded Serial.readStringUntil loop:

Core Task Blocking I/O 0 protocolCoreIngestTask Reads Serial; pushes typed frames to ring (non-blocking xRingbufferSend) 1 m5KernelApplicationCoreTask Drains ring; applies binwire/JSON; never blocks on Serial

See firmware/src/DualCoreHarness.h and ADR 0023.

Registry units of type fast_path_bridge declare execution_core_target (0 or 1) and buffer_allocation_bytes (≥512).

docs.title: Telemetry self-healing loop (Feature 53)
docs.text:
When firmware reports low heap or elevated scheduling jitter, the host AutonomousMitigationEngine automatically validates and injects a binwire throttle patch — closing the loop without manual intervention.

See ADR 0024.

docs.title: Asymmetric Remote Procedure Piping (Features 54–56)
docs.text:
Host HostRPPCompiler emits 10-byte #P frames (!BBHI layout). Core 0 ingests without blocking; Core 1 MicroExecutionKernel applies opcodes (pin HIGH/LOW) without ArduinoJson allocation.

See ADR 0025.

docs.title: Android Host Integration (Features 57–58)
docs.text:
Companion apps under android/ use the USB Host API (FastPathUsbBridge) to emit the same 10-byte ## / #P frames as the Python host. FluxwireMeshNode joins the UDP gossip mesh as android_host_node.

See ADR 0026 and android/README.md.

docs.title: Integrated asymmetric m5-kernel (completion)
docs.text:
Production firmware implements the full dual-core stack via M5IntegratedKernel.h:

Core 0: CrossCorePipe (ring ingest, no JSON heap)
Core 1: M5Kernel → PriorityGatekeeper → BusArbitrator → HandleMemory → ResourceOrchestrator
Host:   HostSchedulerCompiler.apply_to_intent() before transmit

RPP and registry mutations on Core 1 pass through proactive priority-inheritance gates. Do not replace M5Kernel::start() with ad-hoc FreeRTOS ring tasks in main.cpp.

See ADR 0027.

docs.title: m5-jit — native assembly hot-loading
docs.text:
HostJitCompiler cross-compiles vibe C snippets to IRAM-safe .text bytes; M5JitPipeline validates against native_jit schema and can pair with DeltaEncoder registry patches.

Firmware RuntimeLinker allocates MALLOC_CAP_EXEC and executes hooks without reboot.

See ADR 0011 and ADR 0029.

docs.title: Boot sequence
docs.text:
1. registryRuntimeInit() — heartbeat unit, handle pool, bus arbitrator, gatekeeper assets
  2. M5Kernel::start() — ring buffer, Core 0 ingest task, Core 1 application task
  3. Capabilities advertised include m5_central_kernel

docs.title: Unified development lifecycle entry point
docs.text:
The repository exposes a single firmware boot path and a host transmit/observe loop that implements ADR 0001 (Intent-First) and ADR 0002 (Thin Firmware Boundary):

flowchart TB
  subgraph Host["Host plane (Python / Android)"]
    A[Vibe / IDE / Mesh] --> B[HostSchedulerCompiler]
    B --> C[validate_intent_payload]
    C --> D[IntentController.send_intent / binwire]
  end
  subgraph FW0["Core 0 — Protocol Engine"]
    D --> E[CrossCorePipe.protocolCoreIngestTask]
    E --> F[Demux ## #P #M #D #F #S #A #R #I 0xDE]
    F --> G[Lock-free ring push]
  end
  subgraph FW1["Core 1 — Orchestration Engine"]
    G --> H[M5Kernel.dispatchPipeFrame]
    H --> I[PriorityGatekeeper]
    I --> J[BusArbitrator + HandleMemory]
    J --> K[registry_runtime / units]
    K --> L[Telemetry + events uplink]
  end
  L --> A

docs.title: Firmware: m5IntegratedKernelBoot()
docs.text:
Call once from setup() after M5 hardware init (firmware/src/M5IntegratedKernel.cpp):

Stage Module Core Registry + handle pool registryRuntimeInit() prep Jump / vector / health subsystems jumpKernelInitDefaults() … prep Dual-core harness M5Kernel::start() 0 + 1

Do not replace this with ad-hoc Serial.readBytes() tasks or bulk ring pushes in main.cpp. Production ingest lives in CrossCorePipe.cpp (protocolCoreIngestTask on Core 0).

docs.title: Host: IntentController lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Compile — scheduler, delta, binwire, RPP, JIT pipelines
  2. Validate — schema, bus, graph, handles, optional 2PC mesh sync
  3. Transmit — JSON, ##, #P, #M, #D, etc.
  4. Observe — telemetry ECC repair, autofence, agent remediation

See host/m5resolver/kernel_runtime.py (INTEGRATED_BOOT_SEQUENCE, UNIFIED_LIFECYCLE_STAGES).

docs.title: Cross-core lock-free pipe
docs.text:
• CrossCorePipe — RINGBUF_TYPE_NOSPLIT ring between cores
  • Typed frames: 0x01 JSON line, 0x02 binary blob
  • See ADR 0016

docs.title: In-place tokenizer
docs.text:
• Non-JSON declarative payloads (key:value) parsed without heap allocation
  • JSON intents still use ArduinoJson on the Core 1 orchestration path

docs.title: Virtual handle memory
docs.text:
• 32-slot handle table over a 2 KB static pool
  • Compaction via memory_compact intent or supervisor pressure
  • See ADR 0017

docs.title: TDMA bus arbitrator
docs.text:
• Shared I2C/SPI hot-swap gated by time windows per unit
  • bus_arbitration_window_ms in registry units

docs.title: Priority gatekeeper
docs.text:
• Proactive FreeRTOS priority boost before mutex acquisition
  • Registry mutations routed through kGateLockRegistry in the kernel dispatch path
  • See ADR 0019

docs.title: Assembly trampolines & memory overlays
docs.text:
• IRAM #M overlay frames with trampoline hooks
  • See ADR 0015

docs.title: Telemetry Hamming ECC
docs.text:
• Firmware encodes ecc.status_word, ecc.battery_word, ecc.heap_word
  • Host TelemetryECC repairs single-bit corruption before fluxwire patches
  • See ADR 0018

docs.title: Host integration lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Compile — HostSchedulerCompiler injects priority tiers; HostMemoryProfiler preflights pool usage
  2. Validate — validate_intent_payload() schema + bus + graph + handle checks
  3. Transmit — JSON newline, binwire ##, delta 0xDE 0xDA, overlay #M
  4. Observe — telemetry with kernel metrics (kernel_processed_frames, kernel_orchestration_ticks)

docs.title: Key firmware modules
docs.text: Module Path Purpose M5Kernel firmware/src/M5Kernel.* Dual-core orchestration ResourceOrchestrator firmware/src/ResourceOrchestrator.* Pressure-aware staging CrossCorePipe firmware/src/CrossCorePipe.* Core 0 ingest → ring HandleMemory firmware/src/HandleMemory.* Virtual handle pool PriorityGatekeeper firmware/src/PriorityGatekeeper.* Priority inheritance gates BusArbitrator firmware/src/BusArbitrator.* TDMA bus windows registry_runtime firmware/src/registry_runtime.cpp Unit fork + capabilities

docs.text: Port units to registry entries; stop adding per-SKU imperative drivers for new features.

docs.title: Field Graph Compiler (Sanctum / Nodes + Bindings)
docs.text: Some projects use a field graph layout (nodes, bindings, layout) instead of the classic Lego bricks + links format. The host compiler in host/utah_flux/field_compiler.py converts these graphs into device-safe display intents.

docs.title: When to use
docs.text:
Format Keys Studio Compiler Lego bricks bricks, links Utah Flux Studio GUI utah_flux/compiler.py Field graph nodes, bindings External injector or API utah_flux/field_compiler.py

Example: projects/sanctum.flux.json — multi-element UI with IMU-driven label colors.

docs.title: Compile pipeline
docs.text:
1. Detect — is_field_graph_flux() returns true when nodes or bindings are present (and no bricks).
  2. Layout — layout.type (e.g. Sanctum_Voxel_Alpha) selects element placement.
  3. Display intent — nodes become display.elements[] (labels, rects, status chips).
  4. Bindings — wire expressions stay host-side; they are not emitted as firmware registry units.
  5. Validate — validate_intent_payload, validate_intent_safety, HardwareSimulator.simulate_intent.

docs.title: Color handling
docs.text: Hex colors like #FF0000 are converted to RGB565 (0xF800) via rgb888_to_rgb565() — required for M5 display hardware.

docs.title: Host-only bindings
docs.text: Bindings such as imu_coherence → status_label.color are evaluated on the host against live telemetry. They are not compiled into registry blocks on the device (avoids unnecessary sandbox units and CoreS3 crashes).

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: py -m pytest tests/test_field_compiler.py -q

docs.title: Omega Defense Stack (Tier-1 Edge Resiliency)
docs.text: Firmware modules that harden M5Stack CoreS3 devices against timing side-channels, single-node failure, and forensic firmware extraction. Integrated via omegaDefenseInit() and omegaDefenseTick() in firmware/src/OmegaDefense.cpp.

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Module File(s) Role Brownian Shield StochasticShield.* Thermodynamic clock jitter (ADC + TRNG) around registry JSON dispatch Swarm Soul MeshStateMirror.* ESP-NOW Byzantine state vectors every 10ms; ECC checksum; DeepSleep handoff Amnesia Kernel AmnesiaKernel.* Volatile PSRAM payload vault; entropy scrub; IMU geographic anchor Chrono Scheduler ChronoScheduler.* 100µs hyper-tick speculative task commits (motion → delayed alert) Tensor-Void Linkage TensorVoidLinkage.* IRAM 2-bit quantized latent scoring on IMU vector Lazarus Daemon LazarusDaemon.* RTC fast-memory boot counter + resurrection after panic/WDT

docs.title: Boot integration
docs.text:
m5IntegratedKernelBoot()
  └── omegaDefenseInit()
        ├── stochasticShieldInit()
        ├── lazarusDaemonInit()
        ├── amnesiaKernelInit()
        ├── meshStateMirrorInit()
        └── chronoSchedulerInit()

M5Kernel applicationCoreLoop (Core 1)
  └── omegaDefenseTick(tick, frames)
        ├── heap anomaly → mesh suicide handoff
        ├── IMU → amnesia geographic anchor
        ├── mesh broadcast (10ms)
        └── tensor latent score

Registry JSON dispatch on Core 1 is wrapped in StochasticShield::executeWithBrownianJitter.

docs.title: Telemetry (omega_* metrics)
docs.text:
Field Meaning omega_tensor_score Last IRAM latent vector score omega_chrono_commits Speculative tasks committed omega_mesh_peer_updates Valid peer state vectors received omega_mesh_suicide_handoffs DeepSleep handoffs triggered omega_lazarus_boot_count RTC-persisted boot counter omega_amnesia_payload_bytes Bytes in volatile vault

Query capabilities with {"capability_query": true} — includes stochastic_execution_obfuscation, mesh_state_mirror, amnesia_ephemeral_vault, chrono_predictive_scheduler, tensor_void_linkage, lazarus_rtc_resurrection.

docs.title: Ephemeral intent vault
docs.text:
Store mission parameters in volatile RAM only (never flash):

{
  "ephemeral_store": true,
  "ephemeral": { "mission": "sanctum_alpha", "params": { "threshold": 0.8 } }
}

On IMU tamper or geographic drift, the Amnesia kernel entropy-scrubs PSRAM and restarts.

docs.title: Mesh failover (multi-device)
docs.text:
1. Flash two or more CoreS3 units with the same firmware.
  2. ESP-NOW broadcasts state vectors every 10ms on channel 0.
  3. Peer vectors with valid ECC checksum and higher orchestration_tick are adopted.
  4. On critical heap pressure, primary broadcasts final state and enters DeepSleep; peer resumes.

docs.title: Design notes
docs.text:
• No arbitrary code execution from PSRAM — the Amnesia vault stores intent payloads for the existing JSON pipeline, not cast-and-call blobs (unsafe on embedded targets).
  • ESP-NOW degrades gracefully — if radio init fails, single-node execution continues.
  • Lazarus tracks resurrection via esp_reset_reason() + RTC flags, not fragile watchpoints.

docs.title: Omniscient Studio (Auto-Discovery Deck)
docs.text: Lightweight hardware discovery UI without local LLM. Ideal when you only need to see Grove sensors appear on screen after plugging them in.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
pip install -e "host[daemon]"

Double-click launch/Start Omniscient Studio.bat or run:

utah-flux-omniscient

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. HardwareMatrix scans USB for Espressif VID 303A (CoreS3 native USB).
  2. Opens serial at 115200 baud.
  3. Immortal Bootloader emits JSON discovery events on Grove Port A I2C scan.
  4. WebSocket /ws/telemetry streams events to the browser deck.

docs.title: WebSocket events
docs.text: Payload Meaning {"status":"IMMORTAL_KERNEL_LINKED","port":"COMx"} Device connected {"status":"AWAITING_HARDWARE"} No CoreS3 found {"event":"discovery","unit":"ENV_III_SENSOR",...} Sensor plugged in {"event":"disconnect",...} Sensor removed

docs.title: UtahClaw vs Omniscient

Feature Omniscient (:8000) UtahClaw (:8024) Auto USB scan Yes Yes I2C discovery deck Yes Yes Vibe-coding / Ollama No Yes Auto-heal on errors No Yes Canvas UI omniscient.html utah_studio.html

Use Omniscient for classroom sensor demos. Use UtahClaw for full intent-resolution workflows.

docs.title: UtahClaw Omniscient Studio health checks
docs.text:
1. Run Install UtahClaw.bat once (installs host[claw]: FastAPI, Uvicorn, Ollama client).

  3. Double-click Start UtahClaw Studio.bat — browser opens http://127.0.0.1:8024.

  5. Status chip shows NEURAL LINK STABLE (or agent panel connected).
  6. Plug CoreS3 USB — agent panel shows Linked: COMx (close Ghost Forge / serial monitor first).
  7. Attach ENV III on Grove A — hardware deck shows auto-detected unit.
  8. Type intent → MANIFEST — agent panel shows deployed JSON/code.

  5. File issue with logs + project file

docs.title: Reality Substrate (Class-1 Omega)
docs.text:
Firmware modules that dominate physical memory and kinetic matter: RF-kinetic mesh storage, zero-math robotics, ambient IoT compute, phyto-computing, spatial UI, causal debugging, eigen-state compilation, and genesis boot anchoring.

Coordinated by RealitySubstrate — realitySubstrateInit() after sovereignEdgeInit(); realitySubstrateTick() from sovereignEdgeTick().

docs.title: Modules (Disclosures 7–14)
docs.text: Module Role AkashicFileSystem Fragment files into ESP-NOW RF flight; standing-wave rebroadcast (no SD/flash) ChronoKinetic Golden-ratio PWM servo relaxation (no PID) MnemonicProxy Promiscuous WiFi hive scan + ambient ESP-NOW tensor dispatch BiosymmetricBus DAC/ADC organic activation (phyto-computing substrate) SpatialUI Backlight-off magnetic flux projection (intent-gated) CausalDebugger setjmp probability shield; avoided-crash telemetry EigenStateCompiler Flux intent collapse into ephemeral vault GenesisProtocol RTC generation anchor on power-on boot

docs.title: Intent API
docs.text:
{"akashic_inject": true, "akashic_file_id": 42, "akashic": "mission payload"}
{"eigen_collapse": true, "flux_intent": "{...}"}
{"spatial_ui": true}
{"chrono_kinetic": {"target": 45.0}}

Ephemeral vault also accepts ephemeral_store: true (Omega Amnesia kernel).

docs.title: Telemetry (substrate_*)
docs.text: Field Meaning substrate_akashic_fragments RF fragments held in RAM substrate_akashic_rebroadcasts Standing-wave re-energize count substrate_mnemonic_nodes Ambient nodes seen in hive scan substrate_mnemonic_dispatches Parasitic compute tasks sent substrate_biosymmetric_active Phyto bus available substrate_spatial_frames Magnetic projection frames substrate_causal_avoided Crashes avoided by causal shield substrate_eigen_collapses Intent collapse count substrate_genesis_generation RTC boot generation substrate_chrono_pwm Last chrono-kinetic PWM duty

docs.title: ESP-NOW mesh demux
docs.text:
Shared bus (via MeshStateMirror init):

Packet Handler 0xAF magic AkashicFileSystem::RfFragment 0x4D magic MnemonicProxy::ParasiticPayload (outbound) sizeof(ExecutionFrame) SwarmSoul sizeof(MeshStateVector) MeshStateMirror

docs.title: Design notes
docs.text:
• No std::vector — fixed fragment pools for embedded safety
  • SpatialUI dims backlight only when spatial_ui: true intent is sent
  • GenesisProtocol records RTC generation — does not bypass FreeRTOS or bootloader
  • MnemonicProxy scans locally; does not attack third-party devices

docs.title: Sovereign Edge Tier (Declarative Runtime)
docs.text:
Firmware modules that shift CoreS3 from static clock-cycle execution into an adaptive sovereign runtime: phonon-routing acoustic masking, IRAM matrix telematics, and asynchronous swarm conslation over the shared ESP-NOW bus.

Integrated via sovereignEdgeInit() and sovereignEdgeTick() (called from omegaDefenseTick on Core 1).

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Module File(s) Role AcousticMask AcousticMask.* I2S DMA Brownian noise stream on Core 0 (mask_phonon task) MatrixCompute MatrixCompute.* IRAM 2-bit weight lattice telematic vector scoring SwarmSoul SwarmSoul.* ExecutionFrame ESP-NOW conslation (10ms, shared mesh radio)

docs.title: Boot integration
docs.text:
m5IntegratedKernelBoot()
  └── omegaDefenseInit()
  └── sovereignEdgeInit()
        ├── swarmSoulInit()
        └── acousticMaskInit()  → Core 0 phonon task if I2S available

Each orchestration tick:

omegaDefenseTick()
  └── sovereignEdgeTick()
        ├── MatrixCompute::evaluateTelematicVector(IMU + tick)
        └── SwarmSoul::serializeCurrentState()  (10ms, when mesh active)

ESP-NOW receive demux in MeshStateMirror:

  • sizeof(MeshStateVector) → Byzantine mirror failover
  • sizeof(ExecutionFrame) → SwarmSoul peer adoption

docs.title: Telemetry (sovereign_* metrics)
docs.text:
Field Meaning sovereign_matrix_score Last MatrixCompute telematic score sovereign_swarm_adoptions SwarmSoul peer frame adoptions sovereign_phonon_frames I2S DMA frames written sovereign_phonon_active 1 if acoustic mask task running

Capabilities: declarative_phonon_routing, memristive_matrix_compute, swarm_soul_conslation.

docs.title: Platform notes
docs.text:
• AcousticMask uses legacy driver/i2s.h when available. On CoreS3 with M5 codec routing, I2S on GPIO 12/13/14 may be unavailable — the mask degrades gracefully with a serial log (no crash).
  • SwarmSoul does not double-initialize ESP-NOW; it shares the mesh bus initialized by MeshStateMirror.
  • MatrixCompute complements TensorVoidLinkage (3-axis IMU latent) with a 4+ dimension telematic lattice.

docs.title: UtahClaw Omniscient Studio
docs.text: The Intent-Resolution Canvas is a zero-dependency HTML interface that connects to your local UtahClaw daemon over WebSocket. No cloud APIs, no npm build step — just Chromium and loopback TCP.

docs.title: Quick start (recommended)
docs.text:
1. Install once (from repository root): text Install UtahClaw.bat Or manually: pip install -e "./host[claw]" (use repo root path on Windows).
  2. Start Ollama: ollama run llama3
  3. Flash Immortal Bootloader + Omega defense firmware once (see firmware/README.md).
  4. Double-click Start UtahClaw Studio.bat at repo root (or launch/Start UtahClaw Studio.bat).
  5. Browser opens http://127.0.0.1:8024 — keep the UtahClaw Daemon console window open.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Symptom Fix ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED on :8024 Daemon not running — run Install UtahClaw.bat, then Start UtahClaw Studio.bat; check daemon window for ModuleNotFoundError: fastapi Page loads but vibe fails Start ollama run llama3 in another terminal Linked: COMx missing Plug CoreS3 USB; close other serial monitors (only one app per COM port)

docs.title: What you see
docs.text: Panel Purpose Hardware deck Auto-detected Grove units from Immortal I2C discovery Silicon telemetry Raw serial lines from M5Stack UtahClaw console Local Llama-3 responses and auto-heal events Manifest Send plain-English intent → JSON/code → device

docs.title: Standalone HTML file
docs.text:
utah_studio.html at the repository root can be opened directly, but the UtahClaw daemon must be running first:

utah-claw-studio

Then double-click utah_studio.html or open http://127.0.0.1:8024.

When opened as a file:// URL, the canvas defaults to ws://127.0.0.1:8024/ws/studio.

docs.title: WebSocket protocol
docs.text:
Outbound (browser → daemon):

{"type": "vibe_request", "intent": "Make the screen red"}

Inbound (daemon → browser):

type Meaning log Serial telemetry line agent UtahClaw status or generated code discovery Grove sensor attach/detach (data object)

docs.title: Auto-healing loop
docs.text:
When serial output matches error patterns (Traceback, SyntaxError, Guru Meditation, etc.), UtahClaw:

  1. Notifies the agent panel
  2. Sends the error to local Ollama
  3. Pushes corrected JSON or MicroPython paste-mode to the device

docs.title: Privacy and monetization positioning
docs.text:
• 100% offline — schematics and intents never leave the machine
  • Suitable for Sovereign Foundry packaging (PyInstaller .exe + pre-flashed CoreS3 kits)
  • Pro-tier positioning: privacy-focused hardware teams, STEM labs, air-gapped environments

docs.title: Children's Tutorial — Build Your First Device Project
docs.text: Time: 15 minutes You need: A grown-up for USB and the first firmware flash You do not need: To type commands

docs.title: Step 1 — Open Utah Flux Studio
docs.text:
Ask a grown-up to:

  1. Run Install Utah Flux.bat once.
  2. Double-click Start Utah Flux Studio.bat.

A colorful app opens in the browser.

docs.title: Step 2 — Try “Hello Screen”
docs.text:
1. On the left, under Starters, click 👋 Hello Screen.
  2. You will see two bricks on the board connected by a line.
  3. Plug in the M5Stack with USB.
  4. Click 🔌 Connect Device and pick the device (Chrome or Edge).
  5. Click ▶ Play Project.

You should see Hello! on the screen.

docs.title: Step 3 — Build your own idea
docs.text:
1. Drag When Device Tilts onto the board.
  2. Drag Show Message below it.
  3. Click the right white dot on the tilt brick, then the left white dot on the message brick.
  4. Click the message brick and change the text to your name.
  5. Click Play and tilt the device!

docs.title: Step 4 — Add sound
docs.text:
1. Drag Play Sound onto the board.
  2. Connect the tilt brick to the sound brick too (one trigger, two actions).
  3. Pick high pitch in the settings on the right.
  4. Play again — tilt and listen!

docs.title: Step 5 — Save your masterpiece
docs.text: Click 💾 Save Project and choose where to save. Next time, use 📂 Open Project to load it.

docs.title: Brick cheat sheet
docs.text: Brick What it does 🚀 When Project Starts Runs when you press Play 📐 When Device Tilts When you move the device 💬 Show Message Words on screen 🎵 Play Sound Beeps 🎉 Party Mode Fun colors and sound 🛡️ Safe Mode Slows down to protect device

docs.title: If something goes wrong
docs.text:
• No connect button? Use Chrome or Edge, not every browser works.
  • Nothing on screen? Ask a grown-up to check USB and firmware.
  • Read the Live Log on the right — green lines are good, red lines need help.

You built a real hardware project like Lego. Great job!

docs.title: M5Stack Employee Tutorial — Ship Utah Flux as the Default Path
docs.text: Audience: M5Stack staff (engineering, PM, DevRel, support) Goal: Make Utah Flux the standard customer experience in one quarter

docs.title: Week 1 — Align and demo
docs.text:
1. Read m5stack-employee-migration-playbook.md.
  2. Install locally: Install Utah Flux.bat → Start Utah Flux Studio.bat.
  3. Record a 3-minute demo: starter → customize → Play.
  4. Classify your team’s top 20 legacy repos: core / bridge / reference / retire.

docs.title: Week 2 — Content and support
docs.text:
1. Publish customer quickstart (link to docs/en/tutorials/non-technical-tutorial.md).
  2. Replace per-unit “install library X” docs with “add brick / registry entry”.
  3. Train support on:
  4. User never needs CLI
  5. .flux.json is the support artifact to request from customers
  6. Firmware flash is one-time

docs.title: Week 3 — Integration
docs.text:
1. Add your top Units to registry/units.json with bounty_id for community contributions.
  2. Verify compatibility matrix row per shipping board.
  3. Require CI green (quality, analyze, schema-check) on registry changes.

docs.title: Week 4 — Governance
docs.text:
1. Enable branch protection (see github-governance-setup.md).
  2. Track KPIs:
  3. % new features via Utah Flux / registry
  4. Support tickets mentioning “which library”
  5. Time to first working classroom demo

docs.title: Messaging template (external)
docs.text: “M5Stack now ships Utah Flux Studio: build device behavior visually, update without recompiling, and inspect exactly what the device does via the open registry.”

docs.title: Messaging template (internal)
docs.text: “New behavior lands in registry + intents. Legacy repos are frozen except critical fixes until sunset date.”

docs.title: What not to do
docs.text:
• Do not ask beginners to use PlatformIO for each lesson.
  • Do not fork per-SKU C++ drivers for logic that fits registry metadata.
  • Do not bypass safety/simulation gates for “quick” demos.

docs.title: Electronics Engineers Tutorial — Hardware Context Protocol
docs.text: Audience: Hardware and systems engineers Goal: Map physical units into the registry and validate timing/power

docs.title: 1. System boundaries
docs.text: Layer Responsibility Firmware Deterministic IO, telemetry, ACK, registry task supervisor m5resolver Validation, simulation, agentic remediation Utah-Flux Human-facing composition → intents Registry Unit capabilities, addresses, semantic actions

docs.title: 2. Register a new unit
docs.text:
Edit registry/units.json:

{
  "unit_id": "my_sensor",
  "bus": "i2c",
  "address": 72,
  "capabilities": ["temperature"],
  "register_map": { "temp_c": "computed" },
  "max_power_ma": 30,
  "priority": 1,
  "bounty_id": "community-my-sensor-v1"
}

Validate:

  • Schema: schemas/registry.schema.json
  • CI: schema-check workflow

docs.title: 3. Semantic actions (firmware)
docs.text:
Firmware maps registry entries to behaviors in firmware/src/registry_runtime.cpp:

  • ACTION_INDICATE_STATUS_SUCCESS
  • ACTION_INDICATE_ALERT
  • ACTION_REACT_TO_MOTION

Add new semantics in firmware only when a physical primitive is shared across many units.

docs.title: 4. Power and timing gates
docs.text:
Host rejects unsafe configs via host/m5resolver/safety.py:

  • Max unit frequency (Hz)
  • Max estimated power (mA)
  • Speaker frequency/duration limits

Simulation estimates draw in simulation.py before push.

Telemetry schema includes metrics.i2c_bandwidth_pct and metrics.latency_budget_ms for future bus saturation checks.

docs.title: 5. Bring-up checklist
docs.text:
1. Flash firmware, confirm telemetry at 20 Hz.
  2. Send {"capability_query": true}, verify ACK capabilities list.
  3. Push registry slice: {"registry": {"units": {...}}}.
  4. Confirm ACK ok: true and expected physical behavior.
  5. Run agent stress test with examples/agent_loop.py (developer tool).

docs.title: 6. Compatibility
docs.text:
Track board support in compatibility-matrix.md.

CoreS3 is the primary target; other boards need profile entries in firmware/platformio.ini.

docs.title: Non-Technical Tutorial — Run a Class or Workshop
docs.text: Audience: Parents, teachers, facilitators Goal: Run a session without teaching programming

docs.title: Before the session (30 min, adult)
docs.text:
1. Install Python 3.10+ on the computer.
  2. Run Install Utah Flux.bat from the repository.
  3. Flash firmware once from firmware/ (PlatformIO or ask your tech volunteer).
  4. Test: open Start Utah Flux Studio.bat, load Hello Screen, connect device, Play.

docs.title: Part A — Show the idea (10 min)
docs.text:
• “We build with bricks, not code.”
  • Demo: drag When Device Tilts → Show Message + Play Sound, connect dots, Play.

docs.title: Part B — Everyone builds (35 min)
docs.text:
1. Each student opens Utah Flux Studio.
  2. Pick a starter:
  3. Beginners: Hello Screen
  4. Active: Tilt Alarm
  5. Fun: Party Mode
  6. Customize message text and colors in the right panel.
  7. Save project with a clear filename (e.g. class5_alex.flux.json).

docs.title: Part C — Gallery walk (15 min)
docs.text:
• Students demonstrate by tilting or pressing Play.
  • Discuss: “What happens if we connect two actions to one trigger?”

docs.text: Task How Open app Double-click Start Utah Flux Studio.bat Load lesson Open Project Run Connect Device → Play Stop Stop Share work Copy .flux.json file

docs.title: Technical Tutorial — Utah-Flux and m5resolver
docs.text: Audience: Software developers Prerequisites: Python 3.10+, optional PlatformIO for firmware

docs.title: 1. Install and launch
docs.text:
cd host
pip install -e .

Users launch the GUI via Start Utah Flux Studio.bat. Developers can also run:

utah-flux-studio

docs.title: 2. Understand the compile pipeline
docs.text:
1. User builds a project in the GUI (bricks + links).
  2. POST /api/compile calls utah_flux.compiler.compile_project.
  3. Compiler output:
  4. intent — JSON sent to firmware
  5. wires — reactive mappings for live telemetry (browser applies on tilt)
  6. m5resolver.validation + safety + simulation must pass.

Example programmatic compile:

from utah_flux.compiler import compile_project
from utah_flux.templates import get_template

result = compile_project(get_template("tilt_alarm"))
assert result["ok"]
print(result["intent"])

docs.title: 3. Project file format (.flux.json)
docs.text:
{
  "version": 1,
  "name": "My Project",
  "bricks": [
    {"id": "t1", "type": "when_tilt", "x": 60, "y": 60, "params": {}},
    {"id": "a1", "type": "show_message", "x": 60, "y": 200, "params": {"text": "Hi", "color": "green"}}
  ],
  "links": [{"from": "t1", "to": "a1"}]
}

docs.title: 4. Intent protocol (firmware contract)
docs.text:
See schemas/intent.schema.json. Top-level keys:

  • display, speaker, power
  • registry — hot-reload runtime units
  • capability_query — device capability discovery

Send over serial (115200), one JSON object per line.

docs.title: 5. Optional: pyserial controller + agent
docs.text:
For headless or CI bridges:

from m5resolver import IntentController

ctl = IntentController(
    port="COM3",
    registry_path="registry/units.json",
    telemetry_schema_path="schemas/telemetry.schema.json",
    enable_agent=True,
)
ctl.open()
ctl.send_intent({"capability_query": True})
frame = ctl.read_frame()
ctl.close()

docs.title: 6. Add a custom brick
docs.text:
1. Add spec in host/utah_flux/bricks.py (BRICK_CATALOG).
  2. Implement compile logic in host/utah_flux/compiler.py.
  3. Restart studio — palette refreshes from /api/bricks.

docs.title: 7. Run tests
docs.text:
make quality
# or
pytest

docs.title: 8. Dynamic bus multiplexing (v0.3.2)
docs.text:
Registry units can declare protocol types without reflashing:

{
  "registry": {
    "units": {
      "port_a_i2c": {
        "type": "I2C",
        "pins": [21, 22],
        "frequency_hz": 100000
      }
    }
  }
}

Firmware DynamicMultiplexer hot-swaps I2C/SPI/PWM/GPIO. Host bus_validation rejects pin conflicts.

docs.title: 9. Gossip mesh
docs.text:
from m5resolver import FluxwireGossipMesh

mesh = FluxwireGossipMesh(node_id="lab_node_1")
mesh.start_background()
mesh.update_local_registry({"units": []})

Peers discover each other via UDP broadcast and share registry fingerprints.

docs.title: 11. Time-travel debugging (v0.3.3)
docs.text:
Firmware journals intent fingerprints + heap on every registry/intent change. On low heap, it emits:

[FLUXWIRE_TIME_TRAVEL_STREAM]:{"type":"time_travel_journal_dump",...}

Host replay:

from m5resolver import HostReplayEngine

engine = HostReplayEngine()
result = engine.reconstruct_hardware_crash_timeline(journal_json)
if not result.ok:
    print("Fault at", result.fault_fingerprint)

IntentController intercepts journal lines automatically when enable_time_travel=True.

docs.title: 12. Architecture references
docs.text:
• ADR 0001 — Intent-first
  • ADR 0003 — Registry-driven units
  • ADR 0004 — Hardware Context Protocol

docs.title: m5-utah 架构（中文摘要）
docs.text:
Utah Flux Studio / m5-utah 是面向 M5Stack 的意图优先控制平面：主机 Python 将对话与声明式意图编译为线路格式；ESP32 固件通过双核微内核执行。

完整英文版：architecture.md

docs.title: 系统概览
docs.text:
主机 (Python)          串口 / Fluxwire           固件 (ESP32)
IntentController  ──►  CrossCorePipe (Core 0)  ──►  注册表与单元
UtahClaw 画布     ◄──  JSON 发现 / 遥测        ◄──  不朽 I2C 扫描

docs.title: 双核 m5-kernel
docs.text:
核心 角色 Core 0 协议引擎：串口摄取、二进制帧、#A 安全线、不朽 I2C 发现 Core 1 编排引擎：环形缓冲、意图分发、遥测、自愈

setup() 调用 m5IntegratedKernelBoot()；Arduino loop() 挂起，应用逻辑在 Core 1 任务中运行。

docs.title: 不朽引导程序（v0.8+）
docs.text:
刷写一次，永久意图虚拟机。

  • ImmortalDiscovery 每 500ms 扫描 Grove A 口 I2C
  • 发出 discovery / disconnect JSON 事件
  • 主机 HardwareMatrix 自动锁定 Espressif USB（VID 303A）

docs.title: 安全与形式验证
docs.text: 模块 作用 typestate.py 主机预检：非法状态转移拒绝下发 secure_wire.py #A 单调序列防重放 OtaRollbackFence 双缓冲 OTA 回滚

docs.title: 场图编译器（Sanctum / 节点 + 绑定）
docs.text: 部分项目使用场图布局（nodes、bindings、layout），而非经典乐高 bricks + links 格式。主机编译器 host/utah_flux/field_compiler.py 将其转换为设备安全的显示意图。

docs.title: 适用场景
docs.text:
格式 键 工具 编译器 乐高积木 bricks, links Utah Flux Studio GUI utah_flux/compiler.py 场图 nodes, bindings 外部注入器或 API utah_flux/field_compiler.py

示例：projects/sanctum.flux.json — 多元素 UI，IMU 驱动标签颜色。

docs.title: 编译流程
docs.text:
1. 检测 — is_field_graph_flux() 在存在 nodes/bindings 且无 bricks 时为真。
  2. 布局 — layout.type（如 Sanctum_Voxel_Alpha）决定元素位置。
  3. 显示意图 — 节点变为 display.elements[]（标签、矩形、状态芯片）。
  4. 绑定 — 连线表达式保留在主机侧，不编译为固件 registry 单元。
  5. 校验 — validation、safety、simulation 三道关卡。

docs.title: 颜色处理
docs.text: #FF0000 等十六进制颜色通过 rgb888_to_rgb565() 转为 RGB565（0xF800），适配 M5 显示屏硬件。

docs.title: 仅主机绑定
docs.text: 如 imu_coherence → status_label.color 的绑定在主机根据实时遥测求值，不会下发为设备 registry 块（避免多余沙箱单元与 CoreS3 崩溃）。

docs.title: Omega 防御栈（一级边缘韧性）
docs.text: 面向 M5Stack CoreS3 的固件加固模块，用于抵御时序侧信道、单节点故障与固件取证提取。通过 firmware/src/OmegaDefense.cpp 中的 omegaDefenseInit() 与 omegaDefenseTick() 集成。

docs.title: 模块
docs.text: 模块 文件 作用 布朗盾 StochasticShield.* ADC + TRNG 热力学时钟抖动，包裹注册表 JSON 分发 群魂镜像 MeshStateMirror.* ESP-NOW 拜占庭状态向量（10ms）；ECC 校验；DeepSleep 交接 失忆内核 AmnesiaKernel.* 易失 PSRAM 载荷保险库；熵擦除；IMU 地理锚点 时空调度器 ChronoScheduler.* 100µs 超频推测任务提交（运动 → 延迟告警） 张量虚空链路 TensorVoidLinkage.* IRAM 2-bit 量化潜向量对 IMU 评分 拉撒路守护 LazarusDaemon.* RTC 快内存启动计数 + panic/WDT 后复活遥测

docs.title: 启动集成
docs.text:
m5IntegratedKernelBoot()
  └── omegaDefenseInit()
        ├── stochasticShieldInit()
        ├── lazarusDaemonInit()
        ├── amnesiaKernelInit()
        ├── meshStateMirrorInit()
        └── chronoSchedulerInit()

M5Kernel 应用循环 (Core 1)
  └── omegaDefenseTick(tick, frames)

Core 1 上的注册表 JSON 分发由 StochasticShield::executeWithBrownianJitter 包裹。

docs.title: 遥测（omega_* 字段）
docs.text:
字段 含义 omega_tensor_score 最近 IRAM 潜向量分数 omega_chrono_commits 已提交的推测任务数 omega_mesh_peer_updates 收到的有效对端状态向量 omega_mesh_suicide_handoffs 触发的 DeepSleep 交接次数 omega_lazarus_boot_count RTC 持久化启动计数 omega_amnesia_payload_bytes 易失保险库中的字节数

发送 {"capability_query": true} 可查询能力字符串（含 stochastic_execution_obfuscation 等六项）。

docs.title: 易失意图保险库
docs.text:
仅在易失 RAM 中存储任务参数（永不写入 Flash）：

{
  "ephemeral_store": true,
  "ephemeral": { "mission": "sanctum_alpha", "params": { "threshold": 0.8 } }
}

IMU 篡改或地理漂移时，失忆内核对 PSRAM 熵擦除并重启。

docs.title: 网状故障转移（多设备）
docs.text:
1. 为两台及以上 CoreS3 刷入相同固件。
  2. ESP-NOW 每 10ms 广播状态向量。
  3. 校验通过且 orchestration_tick 更高的对端向量被采纳。
  4. 堆内存危急时，主节点广播最终状态并 DeepSleep；对端接续。

docs.title: 设计说明
docs.text:
• 不从 PSRAM 执行任意代码 — 保险库存储 JSON 载荷，供现有意图管道使用。
  • ESP-NOW 优雅降级 — 射频初始化失败时单节点继续运行。
  • 拉撒路 通过 esp_reset_reason() + RTC 标志追踪复活，而非脆弱观察点。

docs.title: 工作原理
docs.text:
1. HardwareMatrix 扫描 USB，查找 Espressif VID 303A（CoreS3 原生 USB）。
  2. 以 115200 波特率打开串口。
  3. 不朽引导程序在 Grove A 口 I2C 扫描时发出 JSON 发现事件。
  4. WebSocket /ws/telemetry 将事件推送到浏览器甲板。

docs.title: WebSocket 事件
docs.text: 载荷 含义 {"status":"IMMORTAL_KERNEL_LINKED","port":"COMx"} 设备已连接 {"status":"AWAITING_HARDWARE"} 未找到 CoreS3 {"event":"discovery","unit":"ENV_III_SENSOR",...} 传感器插入 {"event":"disconnect",...} 传感器拔出

docs.title: UtahClaw 全知工作室健康检查
docs.text:
1. 一次性运行 Install UtahClaw.bat（安装 host[claw]：FastAPI、Uvicorn、Ollama 客户端）。
  2. 本地已运行 ollama run llama3。
  3. 双击 Start UtahClaw Studio.bat → http://127.0.0.1:8024。
  4. UtahClaw Daemon 控制台窗口保持打开（守护进程在此运行）。
  5. 状态显示 NEURAL LINK STABLE。
  6. 插入 CoreS3 USB — 代理面板显示 Linked: COMx（先关闭 Ghost Forge / 串口监视器）。
  7. Grove A 口接 ENV III — 硬件甲板显示自动识别。
  8. 输入意图 → MANIFEST — 代理面板显示已部署 JSON/代码。

docs.title: UtahClaw 故障
docs.text: 问题 处理 ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED 守护进程崩溃 — 查看 Daemon 窗口；重新 Install UtahClaw.bat 缺少 fastapi 在仓库根目录 pip install -e "./host[claw]" COM 口冲突 同一 COM 只能被一个程序占用

docs.text:

  2. 约 50ms 一帧遥测 JSON

docs.title: 现实基底（Class-1 Omega）
docs.text:
主导物理内存与运动物质的固件模块：RF 动能网状存储、零数学机器人学、环境 IoT 计算、植物计算、空间 UI、因果调试、本征态编译与创世启动锚定。

由 RealitySubstrate 协调 — sovereignEdgeInit() 之后调用 realitySubstrateInit()；sovereignEdgeTick() 内调用 realitySubstrateTick()。

docs.title: 模块（披露 7–14）
docs.text: 模块 作用 AkashicFileSystem 文件碎片化为 ESP-NOW 射频飞行；驻波重广播（无 SD/Flash） ChronoKinetic 黄金比例 PWM 舵机松弛（无 PID） MnemonicProxy 混杂模式 WiFi 蜂群扫描 + 环境 ESP-NOW 张量分发 BiosymmetricBus DAC/ADC 有机激活（植物计算基底） SpatialUI 关背光磁通量投影（意图门控） CausalDebugger setjmp 概率护盾；避免崩溃遥测 EigenStateCompiler Flux 意图坍缩进易失保险库 GenesisProtocol 上电启动 RTC 世代锚定

docs.title: 意图 API
docs.text:
{"akashic_inject": true, "akashic_file_id": 42, "akashic": "任务载荷"}
{"eigen_collapse": true, "flux_intent": "{...}"}
{"spatial_ui": true}
{"chrono_kinetic": {"target": 45.0}}

docs.title: 遥测（substrate_*）
docs.text: 见英文版字段表 reality-substrate.md（中英同步）。

docs.title: ESP-NOW 网状解复用
docs.text: 包类型 处理器 0xAF Akashic 碎片 0x4D Mnemonic 任务（出站） ExecutionFrame SwarmSoul MeshStateVector MeshStateMirror

docs.title: 设计说明
docs.text:
• 无 std::vector — 固定碎片池
  • SpatialUI 仅在 spatial_ui: true 时关闭背光
  • GenesisProtocol 记录 RTC 世代 — 不绕过 FreeRTOS
  • MnemonicProxy 仅本地扫描

docs.title: 相关文档
docs.text:
• 主权边缘层
  • Omega 防御栈
  • ADR 0047

docs.title: 主权边缘层（声明式运行时）
docs.text:
将 CoreS3 从静态时钟周期陷阱转为自适应主权运行时的固件模块：声子路由声学掩蔽、IRAM 矩阵遥测、以及共享 ESP-NOW 总线上的异步群魂同步。

通过 sovereignEdgeInit() 与 sovereignEdgeTick() 集成（在 Core 1 的 omegaDefenseTick 中调用）。

docs.title: 模块
docs.text: 模块 文件 作用 AcousticMask AcousticMask.* Core 0 I2S DMA 布朗噪声流（mask_phonon 任务） MatrixCompute MatrixCompute.* IRAM 2-bit 权重格点遥测向量评分 SwarmSoul SwarmSoul.* ExecutionFrame ESP-NOW 同步（10ms，共享网状射频）

docs.title: 启动集成
docs.text:
m5IntegratedKernelBoot()
  └── sovereignEdgeInit()
        ├── swarmSoulInit()
        └── acousticMaskInit()  → I2S 可用时启动 Core 0 声子任务

docs.title: 遥测（sovereign_* 字段）
docs.text:
字段 含义 sovereign_matrix_score 最近 MatrixCompute 遥测分数 sovereign_swarm_adoptions SwarmSoul 对端帧采纳次数 sovereign_phonon_frames I2S DMA 已写帧数 sovereign_phonon_active 声学掩蔽任务是否运行（1/0）

能力字符串：declarative_phonon_routing、memristive_matrix_compute、swarm_soul_conslation。

docs.title: 平台说明
docs.text:
• AcousticMask 在可用时使用 legacy driver/i2s.h。CoreS3 若 M5 编解码器占用 I2S 引脚，掩蔽会优雅降级（仅日志，不崩溃）。
  • SwarmSoul 不重复初始化 ESP-NOW，与 MeshStateMirror 共享网状总线。
  • MatrixCompute 与 TensorVoidLinkage（三轴 IMU 潜向量）互补，支持更高维遥测格点。

docs.title: 相关文档
docs.text:
• Omega 防御栈
  • 固件 README
  • ADR 0046

docs.title: 技术栈（v0.8.2）
docs.text: 组件 路径 作用 Utah Flux Studio host/utah_flux/studio.py 乐高 IDE（8765） UtahClaw 画布 host/utah_flux/static/utah_studio.html 意图解析 UI（8024） 全知甲板 host/utah_flux/omniscient_daemon.py 自动发现（8000） UtahClaw 守护进程 host/utah_flux/utahclaw_daemon.py Ollama + 串口桥 场图编译器 host/utah_flux/field_compiler.py Sanctum nodes/bindings → 显示意图 m5resolver host/m5resolver/ 意图、类型态、安全线 Omega 防御栈 firmware/src/OmegaDefense.* 等 边缘韧性模块 主权边缘层 firmware/src/SovereignEdge.* 等 声明式运行时 现实基底 firmware/src/RealitySubstrate.* 等 Class-1 Omega 不朽发现固件 firmware/src/ImmortalDiscovery.* Core 0 I2C 扫描 双核内核 firmware/ M5Kernel 运行时

docs.title: 用户与开发者
docs.text:
• 儿童/创客： Start Utah Flux Studio.bat
  • 氛围编程工程师： Install UtahClaw.bat → Start UtahClaw Studio.bat + Ollama
  • 发现演示： launch/Start Omniscient Studio.bat
  • 开发者： pip install -e host、pytest、examples/
  • Sanctum 场图： projects/sanctum.flux.json（见 field-graph-compiler.md）

docs.title: 完整教程
docs.text: 技术用户教程

docs.title: 意图模型
docs.text: 键：display、speaker、power、registry、capability_query、ephemeral_store 见 schemas/intent.schema.json

docs.title: 安全与仿真
docs.text: 编译前经过 validation、safety、simulation。

docs.title: Studio API
docs.text:
• GET /api/bricks
  • GET /api/templates
  • POST /api/compile

docs.title: 遗留 CLI
docs.text: m5resolver CLI 与 examples/ 供自动化使用，产品用户不需要。

docs.title: UtahClaw 全知工作室
docs.text: 意图解析画布（Intent-Resolution Canvas） 是一个零依赖 HTML 界面，通过 WebSocket 连接本地 UtahClaw 守护进程。无需云端 API，无需 npm 构建 — 只需 Chromium 浏览器和本机回环网络。

docs.title: 快速开始（推荐）
docs.text:
1. 一次性安装（在仓库根目录）： text Install UtahClaw.bat 或手动：pip install -e "./host[claw]"（Windows 请从仓库根目录执行）。
  2. 启动 Ollama：ollama run llama3
  3. 固件只需刷写一次（含 Omega 防御栈，见 firmware/README.md）。
  4. 双击仓库根目录 Start UtahClaw Studio.bat（或 launch/Start UtahClaw Studio.bat）。
  5. 浏览器打开 http://127.0.0.1:8024 — 保持 UtahClaw Daemon 控制台窗口运行。

docs.title: 故障排除
docs.text: 现象 处理 :8024 显示 ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED 守护进程未启动 — 运行 Install UtahClaw.bat 后 Start UtahClaw Studio.bat；检查窗口是否有 fastapi 缺失错误 页面能开但 vibe 失败 另开终端运行 ollama run llama3 无 Linked: COMx 插入 CoreS3 USB；关闭占用串口的其他程序

docs.title: 界面说明
docs.text: 面板 作用 硬件甲板 不朽 I2C 自动发现的 Grove 模块 硅遥测 M5Stack 原始串口输出 UtahClaw 控制台 本地 Llama-3 回复与自动修复事件 MANIFEST 用自然语言发送意图 → JSON/代码 → 设备

docs.title: 独立 HTML 文件
docs.text:
仓库根目录的 utah_studio.html 可直接打开，但须先启动 UtahClaw 守护进程：

utah-claw-studio

然后双击 utah_studio.html 或访问 http://127.0.0.1:8024。

以 file:// 打开时，画布默认连接 ws://127.0.0.1:8024/ws/studio。

docs.title: WebSocket 协议
docs.text:
浏览器 → 守护进程：

{"type": "vibe_request", "intent": "让屏幕变红"}

守护进程 → 浏览器：

type 含义 log 串口遥测行 agent UtahClaw 状态或生成代码 discovery Grove 传感器插拔（data 对象）

docs.title: 自动修复循环
docs.text:
当串口输出匹配错误模式（Traceback、SyntaxError、Guru Meditation 等）时，UtahClaw 会：

  1. 在代理面板通知用户
  2. 将错误发给本地 Ollama
  3. 向设备推送修正后的 JSON 或 MicroPython 粘贴模式代码

docs.title: 隐私与商业化定位
docs.text:
• 完全离线 — 原理图与意图不离开本机
  • 适合 Sovereign Foundry 打包（PyInstaller .exe + 预刷 CoreS3 套件）
  • 面向注重隐私的团队、STEM 实验室、隔离网络环境

docs.title: 相关文档
docs.text:
• 全知发现工作室 — 仅硬件发现（无 LLM）
  • 架构 — 不朽引导程序与双核内核
  • ADR 0044

docs.title: 儿童教程 — 搭建第一个设备项目
docs.text: 时间： 约 15 分钟 需要： 大人帮忙接 USB 和第一次烧录固件 不需要： 输入命令

docs.title: 第 1 步 — 打开 Utah Flux Studio
docs.text:
请大人帮你：

  1. 运行一次 Install Utah Flux.bat
  2. 双击 Start Utah Flux Studio.bat

浏览器会打开彩色应用。

docs.title: 第 2 步 — 试试“Hello Screen”
docs.text:
1. 在左边 Starters 点击 👋 Hello Screen
  2. 画布上会出现两个连好的积木
  3. 用 USB 连接 M5Stack
  4. 点击 🔌 Connect Device（用 Chrome 或 Edge）
  5. 点击 ▶ Play Project

屏幕上应显示 Hello!

docs.title: 第 3 步 — 自己做
docs.text:
1. 拖 When Device Tilts 到画布
  2. 拖 Show Message 到下面
  3. 点倾斜积木右边的白点，再点消息积木左边的白点
  4. 点消息积木，在右边把文字改成你的名字
  5. 点 Play，倾斜设备！

docs.title: 第 4 步 — 加声音
docs.text:
1. 拖 Play Sound 到画布
  2. 把倾斜积木也连到声音积木
  3. 在右边选 high 音调
  4. 再 Play，边倾斜边听！

docs.title: 第 5 步 — 保存作品
docs.text: 点 💾 Save Project 保存。下次用 📂 Open Project 打开。

docs.title: 积木速查
docs.text: 积木 作用 🚀 When Project Starts 按 Play 时运行 📐 When Device Tilts 倾斜时触发 💬 Show Message 屏幕文字 🎵 Play Sound 发声 🎉 Party Mode 欢乐模式 🛡️ Safe Mode 保护设备

docs.title: 出问题怎么办
docs.text:
• 不能连接？用 Chrome 或 Edge
  • 屏幕没反应？请大人检查 USB 和固件
  • 看右边 Live Log，绿色好，红色要找大人

你像搭乐高一样完成了硬件项目，真棒！

docs.title: M5Stack 员工教程 — 推广 Utah Flux 为标准路径
docs.text: 对象： 工程、产品、DevRel、支持 目标： 一个季度内让 Utah Flux 成为默认客户体验

docs.title: 第 1 周
docs.text:
1. 阅读 员工迁移手册
  2. 本地安装并录制 3 分钟演示
  3. 盘点前 20 个旧仓库：核心/桥接/参考/退役

docs.title: 第 2 周
docs.text:
1. 发布中英快速入门
  2. 文档从“安装库 X”改为“添加积木/注册表项”
  3. 培训支持：用户不需 CLI，索要 .flux.json

docs.title: 第 3 周
docs.text:
1. 补齐 registry/units.json 与 bounty_id
  2. 更新兼容性矩阵
  3. 注册表变更走 CI

docs.title: 第 4 周
docs.text:
1. 启用分支保护
  2. 跟踪 KPI：新功能占比、支持单类型、课堂首次 Play 时间

docs.title: 对外话术
docs.text: “M5Stack 推出 Utah Flux Studio：可视化搭建、无需反复编译，设备行为在开放注册表中一目了然。”

docs.title: 对内话术
docs.text: “新功能只进 Utah Flux 与注册表；旧仓库仅关键修复直至退场。”

docs.title: 避免
docs.text:
• 让初学者每节课用 PlatformIO
  • 为可用注册表表达的差异再写 C++ 分叉驱动
  • 绕过安全/仿真门禁做演示

docs.title: 电子工程师教程 — 硬件上下文协议
docs.text: 对象： 硬件/系统工程师 目标： 在注册表中描述物理单元并验证功耗/时序

docs.title: 1. 分层边界
docs.text: 层 职责 固件 确定性 IO、遥测、ACK、注册表任务监管 m5resolver 校验、仿真、Agent 修复 Utah-Flux 可视化 → 意图 注册表 能力、地址、语义动作

docs.title: 2. 注册新 Unit
docs.text: 编辑 registry/units.json，参考 schemas/registry.schema.json。

docs.title: 3. 语义动作
docs.text:
在 firmware/src/registry_runtime.cpp 中映射，例如：

  • ACTION_INDICATE_STATUS_SUCCESS
  • ACTION_REACT_TO_MOTION

docs.title: 4. 安全门禁
docs.text: host/m5resolver/safety.py 限制频率、功耗、扬声器参数。 simulation.py 在下发前估算电流。

docs.title: 5. 上电检查清单
docs.text:
1. 烧录固件，确认 20Hz 遥测
  2. 发送 capability_query
  3. 下发 registry 片段
  4. 确认 ACK 与物理行为

docs.title: 6. 兼容性
docs.text: 见 兼容性矩阵。

docs.title: 非技术用户教程 — 上课或办工作坊
docs.text: 对象： 家长、老师、活动组织者 目标： 不教编程也能带完整一节课

docs.title: 课前准备（约 30 分钟，成人）
docs.text:
1. 安装 Python 3.10+
  2. 运行 Install Utah Flux.bat
  3. 在 firmware/ 烧录一次固件
  4. 测试：打开 Studio → Hello Screen → 连接 → Play

docs.title: A 部分 — 演示（10 分钟）
docs.text:
• “我们用积木，不用写代码。”
  • 演示：倾斜 → 文字 + 声音，连线，Play

docs.title: B 部分 — 动手（35 分钟）
docs.text:
1. 每人打开 Utah Flux Studio
  2. 选模板：入门 Hello / 活动 Tilt Alarm / 欢乐 Party
  3. 改文字和颜色
  4. Save Project 保存

docs.title: C 部分 — 展示（15 分钟）
docs.text: 学生演示作品，讨论“一个触发连多个动作”。

docs.title: 日常用法
docs.text: 任务 操作 打开 双击 Start Utah Flux Studio.bat 加载 Open Project 运行 Connect Device → Play 停止 Stop 分享 复制 .flux.json 文件

docs.title: 不需要命令行
docs.text: 所有操作都在 Utah Flux Studio 里完成。

docs.title: 获取帮助
docs.text:
• 应用内 Live Log
  • 运维手册
  • projects/ 示例项目

docs.title: 技术用户教程 — Utah-Flux 与 m5resolver
docs.text: 对象： 软件开发人员 前提： Python 3.10+，可选 PlatformIO

docs.title: 1. 安装与启动
docs.text:
cd host
pip install -e .

终端用户双击 Start Utah Flux Studio.bat。开发者也可：

utah-flux-studio

docs.title: 2. 编译流水线
docs.text:
1. GUI 中搭建积木与连线
  2. POST /api/compile → utah_flux.compiler.compile_project
  3. 输出 intent（下发设备）与 wires（遥测联动）
  4. 必须通过 validation、safety、simulation

代码示例：

from utah_flux.compiler import compile_project
from utah_flux.templates import get_template

result = compile_project(get_template("tilt_alarm"))
assert result["ok"]

docs.title: 3. 项目格式 .flux.json
docs.text: 含 bricks（积木列表）与 links（连线 from/to）。

docs.title: 4. 意图协议
docs.text: 见 schemas/intent.schema.json。 串口 115200，每行一个 JSON。

docs.title: 5. 可选：串口控制器
docs.text:
from m5resolver import IntentController

ctl = IntentController(port="COM3", registry_path="registry/units.json", enable_agent=True)
ctl.open()
ctl.send_intent({"capability_query": True})
ctl.close()

docs.title: 6. 添加自定义积木
docs.text:
1. host/utah_flux/bricks.py
  2. host/utah_flux/compiler.py
  3. 重启 Studio

docs.title: 7. 测试
docs.text: pytest
